heroHeadline: Day 36: Operativo Enjambre Momentum Builds; Argentina CJNG Designation Takes Hold
heroSubtitle: Day 36 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa. Operativo Enjambre has produced dozens of CJNG-linked official arrests across Jalisco and Michoacán — including Tequila's mayor (March 29) — systematically dismantling the cartel's municipal-level protection network. O3 (Juan Carlos Valencia González) continues consolidating leadership under a collective council structure, while El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) remains the primary high-value target with a $5 million U.S. bounty. Argentina's FTO designation of CJNG (March 26) — the third such designation after the U.S. and Canada — expands the multilateral legal framework against the cartel's 40+ country footprint. FIFA World Cup 2026 security deployment of 100,000 personnel continues in Guadalajara.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, NCTC, InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Wilson Center, Reuters, AP, Wall Street Journal, Al Jazeera, UPI, Excélsior, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, Janes, Chatham House, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: March 30, 2026.
